skills.name: adjust-inventory
skills.title: Adjust Inventory
skills.description: Adjust the on-hand quantity of a product at a warehouse in SKU.io — an increase, a decrease, or a set-to-value. Use this for stock takes, corrections, damage/shrinkage write-offs, or found stock. Resolve the product with find-product first so the adjustment targets the right product and warehouse.
skills.tagline: Increase / decrease / set on-hand stock at a warehouse

skills.name: create-sales-order
skills.title: Create a Sales Order
skills.description: Create a new sales order in SKU.io with one or more line items. Use this to place an order for a customer — capturing the order status, order date, optional customer and store, and the SKUs, quantities, and unit prices being sold. Resolve products first with find-product so each line references a real product.
skills.tagline: Create a sales order with line items

skills.name: connect-to-sku
skills.title: Connect to SKU.io
skills.description: The entry point for any SKU.io task. Establish an authenticated connection: point at the correct tenant base URL, send a Personal Access Token as a Bearer token, verify it works with a cheap identity call, and inspect the token's scopes so you know what you are allowed to do. Use this first whenever you are about to call the SKU.io API.
skills.tagline: Authenticate, verify the token, and check scopes before anything else

skills.name: create-saved-view
skills.title: Create a Saved View (and favorite it)
skills.description: Create a saved view for any SKU.io data table (products, sales orders, inventory, …) — a named, reusable configuration of visible columns, filters, sort, and search — and favorite it so it appears on the table's favorites bar. Use this to give a user a one-click way back to a specific slice of data: a fresh import, an exception queue, a curated report. Composable — other skills (e.g. build-product-catalog) call it to leave behind a view of what they just created.
skills.tagline: Create and favorite a saved view — columns, filters, sort — on any data table

skills.name: sync-sops
skills.title: Sync your SOPs with SKU.io changes
skills.description: Keep your internal Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) in step with SKU.io. SKU.io ships often — menus move, workflows change, features are added — which quietly breaks the step-by-step procedures your team relies on. Give this skill your SOP content plus the date you last reviewed it: it reads SKU.io's public changelog and documentation, finds the changes that affect your procedures, and rewrites the exact stale steps — or drafts a brand-new SOP from the current docs, in your own format. Use it whenever internal docs/SOPs may be out of date, or on a schedule to catch drift.
skills.tagline: Reconcile your internal SOPs against what changed in SKU.io — automatically

skills.name: build-product-catalog
skills.title: Build a Product Catalog from Any Source
skills.description: Turn product information from any source — a spreadsheet or CSV, a Google Sheet, a supplier price list, a website or product page, or pasted text — into products in SKU.io. Extract the raw rows, map them onto SKU.io's product fields, de-duplicate against the existing catalog, then create the products. Use this to onboard a new catalog, import a supplier's range, or bulk-load products from an external system.
skills.tagline: Import products from a spreadsheet, price list, web page, or pasted text

skills.name: find-product
skills.title: Find a Product
skills.description: Look up a product in the SKU.io catalog by SKU, barcode, or a free-text search over name and SKU. Use this whenever you need to resolve a product reference to its SKU.io product record and id — for example before adding it to a sales order, checking stock, or reporting on it.
skills.tagline: Resolve a product by SKU, barcode, or fuzzy search

skills.name: create-purchase-order
skills.title: Create a Purchase Order
skills.description: Create a purchase order in SKU.io — a replenishment order placed with a supplier — from either a plain description of what to buy or a source document (a supplier email, an order-form spreadsheet, a demand-plan export) that supplies the SKUs and quantities. Resolves the supplier, destination warehouse, unit costs, currency, and (optionally) payment term, builds the line items, and creates the PO as a draft or an open order. Covers standard and consignment replenishment. Resolve products with find-product first so every line references a real product.
skills.tagline: Create a replenishment PO for a supplier from a description or document

skills.name: edit-packing-slip-template
skills.title: Edit a Packing-Slip PDF Template
skills.description: Edit a SKU.io packing-slip PDF template with the packing-specific rules that keep the footer banner, logo, and per-store layouts consistent. Use this when changing a packing slip's footer banner or branding, or when the banner renders inconsistently — present on some orders or stores, missing or clipped on others. Enforces the exact footer-banner structure the PDF export requires to pin a banner to every page, the banner aspect ratio, cross-store consistency, and a multi-order visual QA pass against real orders. Extends edit-pdf-template — do the base edit/validate/save loop there; this adds the packing rules.
skills.tagline: Edit packing-slip templates without breaking the footer banner

skills.name: edit-pdf-template
skills.title: Edit a PDF Template
skills.description: Edit any SKU.io PDF document template — packing slips, quotes, sales orders, pick lists, purchase orders, and vendor/sales credits — by editing its HTML content directly, then validating, previewing against real and mock documents, and visually QA-ing the rendered PDF before saving. Use this to change branding, layout, a logo or footer banner, or which fields a document shows. Runs a deterministic read → edit → validate → preview → verify → save loop; every save snapshots a version you can restore, so edits are reversible. Prefer this over the in-app AI builder, which is the fallback for users without an agent. For packing slips, load edit-packing-slip-template too.
skills.tagline: Edit, preview, and visually QA any PDF document template — safely
